title: potentilla inclinata var. johanniniana
intro: Potentilla inclinata var. johanniniana, also known as Johann's cinquefoil, is a plant species within the rosaceae family, characterized by its unique, creeping habit and clusters of five-petaled flowers. Traditionally, Johann's cinquefoil has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Johann's cinquefoil can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
